text.title: Research Repository Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Research Repository Setup methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Platform selection MUST score >=3 candidates on 5 criteria (API maturity, AI tagging, integrations, cost/user, ZDR support).
rule.rationale: Single-vendor pick without scoring locks the team into a bad fit.

rule.statement: Starter taxonomy MUST have 30-80 tags grouped by class (segment, pain, JTBD, behavior).
rule.rationale: <30 tags is too coarse; >80 is unmanageable before AI tagging arrives.

rule.statement: Access matrix MUST define exactly 3 roles (admin, researcher, read-only) at setup time; finer roles emerge later.
rule.rationale: More roles upfront produces permission sprawl.

rule.statement: Setup MUST include a 90-day backfill of historical studies so the repo is useful from day 1.
If AI tagging will run on transcripts, ZDR-eligible endpoints MUST be configured before any backfill.
rule.rationale: Backfill that violates ZDR is a compliance liability.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
